rationale: Both correctly diagnose the #4527 regression (non-async-chunk shipping (None, payload) starves accumulate_full_payload_output) and correctly point to #4792/rebase commit a560ed18 as the fix, matching the thread. Y hews closer to the ground-truth evidence, quoting the code comment nearly verbatim and correctly noting #4792 was 'closed as conflicting, never directly merged' but cherry-picked into the rebase — consistent with akshatvishu's odd 'for some reason' phrasing — whereas X flatly asserts the PR 'is merged,' which conflicts with that nuance. X also pads its answer with unverifiable specifics (a fabricated-sounding '(300s connector-input timeout)' inserted into a quoted code comment, an invented #4962 reference, and a 600s-vs-300s timeout claim) that aren't grounded in the provided thread, which is a hallucination risk. Both give equally actionable workarounds/fixes and verification guidance, so completeness is roughly tied.
